name: Curtido
subtitle: El Salvador's lightly fermented cabbage relish
blrNote: All ingredients available at any BLR market. Quick ferment - ready in 24 hours. Pairs beautifully with rice, chapati, or as a side to any South Indian meal.

IN.where: Supermarkets like More, Spar, or Nature's Basket. Or online.

equipment.name: Glass jar or container with lid

tldr: Shred cabbage and carrot, salt, massage, add oregano, pack. Ready in 24 hours. Eat with everything.

steps.title: Shred the vegetables
steps.instruction: Finely shred cabbage, julienne carrots, thinly slice onion. Combine in a large bowl.
tips: A mandoline slicer gets the cabbage paper-thin in seconds - worth the small investment
Save the tough outer cabbage leaves to use as a weight pressing down on the packed jar

steps.title: Salt and massage
steps.instruction: Sprinkle salt over the vegetables. Massage for 3–5 minutes until they soften and release liquid.
tips: It doesn't need to release as much liquid as sauerkraut - curtido is lighter
Taste the veg after massaging - if it's too salty now, it'll be too salty later, so adjust before packing
Squeeze handfuls firmly like you're wringing a towel - the cell walls break down faster that way

steps.title: Add flavour and pack
steps.instruction: Add oregano and optional vinegar. Toss well. Pack tightly into a jar. Press down so liquid (and vinegar if using) covers the veg.
tips: Vinegar speeds the sour flavour but isn't needed if fermenting longer
Crush the dried oregano between your palms before adding - it releases far more aroma
Pack in layers and press down after each handful to eliminate air pockets

steps.title: Ferment briefly
steps.instruction: Cover loosely. Leave at room temperature. In BLR, 24 hours gives a lightly sour, fresh curtido. 48–72 hours for more tang.
tips: Traditional Salvadoran curtido is only lightly fermented - it's not as sour as sauerkraut
Press the veg below the liquid line each time you check - exposed bits can develop off-flavours
If you see tiny bubbles rising through the jar, fermentation is working perfectly

steps.title: Refrigerate and serve
steps.instruction: Refrigerate when it reaches your preferred sourness. Keeps 2–3 weeks.
tips: The brine left in the jar is liquid gold - splash it on tacos, rice bowls, or use as a salad dressing base
Curtido gets softer each day in the fridge, so eat it within the first week if you love crunch
Pile it on fried eggs, grilled cheese, or dal rice - it works far beyond pupusas

images.caption: Curtido
images.attribution: Photo by Brooke Lark on Unsplash
images.alt: Curtido - El Salvador's lightly fermented cabbage relish

culturalContext.story: Curtido is El Salvador's national condiment - an essential partner to pupusas (thick corn cakes stuffed with cheese, beans, or chicharrón). It's lighter and faster than sauerkraut by design: Salvadoran cuisine tends toward fresh, bright flavours rather than deeply soured preservation.
culturalContext.historicalNote: Central American fermented vegetables predate Spanish colonisation. Curtido as we know it evolved after European cabbage was introduced in the colonial period, blending indigenous preservation techniques with Old World vegetables.
culturalContext.significance: Curtido demonstrates that fermentation doesn't have to be long or intense to be valuable. Short ferments develop flavour and begin beneficial bacterial activity without crossing into strong sourness - ideal for people new to fermented foods.
culturalContext.funFact: In El Salvador, no pupusa is considered complete without curtido and salsa roja. The combination is so standard that pupuserías serve both as automatically as a restaurant serves bread and butter in France.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Very quick in BLR heat
thingsToAccountFor.description: At 25–30°C, curtido ferments quickly. Taste after 18 hours - you may find it's already at your preferred sourness.

thingsToAccountFor.title: Texture softens over time
thingsToAccountFor.description: The longer it ferments, the softer the texture. Traditional curtido retains some crunch - don't overferment if you prefer crispness.

dehydratorIntegration.method: Drain, spread thinly, dehydrate at 40°C for 6–8 hours.
dehydratorIntegration.result: Tangy vegetable flakes - crumble on rice or use as seasoning.
tips: Better when only lightly fermented before dehydrating

variations.name: Spiced curtido
variations.description: Add sliced jalapeño or green chilli. A natural fit for Indian palates.

variations.name: Beet curtido
variations.description: Add 1 small grated beetroot. Vivid pink colour, earthy sweetness.

videos.title: How to Make Curtido - Salvadoran Fermented Slaw
